data.title: CZ Rules Out Return to Binance After Trump Pardon, Marks Definitive Exit from Crypto Empire
data.description:
Changpeng Zhao Rules Out Binance Return After Trump Pardon

Binance co-founder Changpeng Zhao has ruled out any return to the crypto exchange he helped build, despite receiving a presidential pardon from U.S. President Donald Trump that removed the legal barriers preventing his involvement.

Speaking on CNBC’s Squawk Box, Zhao said the pardon fully lifted restrictions tied to his November 2023 guilty plea for failing to maintain an effective anti–money laundering program at Binance. Still, he made clear that a comeback was neither necessary nor desirable.

“At the time, it was very painful. I didn't like it. But after, you get used to it. I don't think it was good for me to go back. We should leave room for other strong leaders to grow.”

A candid conversation from Davos - on prison, pardon, and what freedom means going forward.

Full interview on @CNBC with @andrewrsorkin. Focused on building what’s next. pic.twitter.com/x94llJFac2

— CZ 🔶 BNB (@cz_binance) January 25, 2026

Zhao pleaded guilty in late 2023 and later served a four-month prison sentence, alongside a prohibition on working at Binance. Trump’s pardon in October reopened the possibility of his return, drawing scrutiny from U.S. lawmakers given Binance’s past compliance failures and its proximity to Trump-linked crypto ventures—claims the president denied having prior knowledge of.

DON THE GRIFTER: The hypocrisy is stunning—Trump pardons Binance billionaire CZ for massive money laundering, then claims on 60 Minutes, "I don't even know who he is."

This denial playbook? Straight from his mentor Roy Cohn: deny, deny, deny; never admit you're wrong—you're… pic.twitter.com/tHXftBYKVH

— Popular Liberal 🇺🇸 (@PopularLiberal) November 6, 2025

Despite the renewed attention, Zhao framed his exit as final. He said returning would risk undermining the leadership team now running the exchange, adding that Binance no longer needs his direct involvement.

Zhao pointed to Binance’s continued growth under current leadership, led by CEO Richard Teng and long-time executive Yi He, as evidence that the company has stabilized since his departure.

In a December open letter, Binance disclosed that its user base had surpassed 300 million, with $34 trillion in total trading volume recorded for the year—figures Zhao cited as confirmation that the transition has not slowed the exchange’s momentum.

I’ve been a #Binance fan since day one, since the moment I discovered crypto.

I still remember the early days when demand was so high that people were literally selling Binance accounts from user to user just to get access.

Fast forward to today:

$34T traded in 2025.
20M+… pic.twitter.com/fRXlI6vZWR

— Chihiro (@CryptoChihiro) January 26, 2026

While remaining a shareholder, Zhao described himself as largely hands-off, offering occasional public commentary rather than operational input.

“I just thought, look;they don't neex a backseat driver today. I'm still a shareholder. When I want to give advice, I just write it on Twitter.”

CZ Looks Beyond Binance to Bitcoin’s Next Phase

With crypto markets under pressure at the start of 2026, Zhao used the interview to share a broader market view, predicting that Bitcoin could enter a new supercycle within the next year.

🗣 Fidelity suggests that #BTC may have entered a “Supercycle”

Long-term adoption, structural demand, and reduced sell pressure could change Bitcoin’s traditional market cycles.

Is this the cycle that breaks the pattern? 👀📈 pic.twitter.com/ySN9Vo8jzN

— Cryptocurrency Inside (@Crypto_Inside_) January 12, 2026

He argued that shifting political winds—particularly the U.S. government’s increasingly pro-crypto stance—could upend Bitcoin’s traditional four-year market rhythm.

“Historically, every four years there’s an all-time high and then a drop. But this time, with the U.S. being so pro crypto and other countries following, I think we will probably break the four-year cycle.”

If realized, such a shift would mark not just a turning point for Bitcoin, but a symbolic one for Zhao himself—closing the chapter on Binance while positioning him as an observer of crypto’s next institutional phase rather than its chief architect.

data.title: South Korea’s Coinone Considers Equity Sale Amid Losses and $52 Million Valuation, Coinbase Among Potential Buyers
data.description:
Coinone, the third-largest cryptocurrency exchange in South Korea, has reportedly entered the shop window as a wave of consolidation sweeps through one of the world’s most active digital asset hubs.

Chairman Cha Myung-hoon is looking to offload a controlling interest in the platform, a move that has immediately triggered talk of a potential entry by American giant Coinbase.

Who Is Looking To Buy Coinone?

Speculation is mounting that Coinbase, the leading US-based exchange, is eyeing an equity stake in the Seoul-based firm.

📣 NEW: #Coinbase is weighing a potential equity investment in South Korea’s #Coinone. pic.twitter.com/nP6oVu64we

— Coinpaper (@coinpapercom) January 26, 2026

Industry sources suggest that Coinbase executives are scheduled to visit South Korea this week to hold high-level discussions with Coinone and other local players.

While Coinbase currently operates in over 100 countries, it has yet to establish a regulated presence in South Korea’s unique and strictly governed market.

When asked about the potential deal, a Coinbase spokesperson stated,

“we don’t comment on rumours or speculation.”

For its part, Coinone has acknowledged the ongoing search for partners, noting, “we are discussing partnerships, including equity investments, with overseas exchanges and domestic financial institutions,” though they clarified that no final agreement has been reached.

Why Is The Chairman Selling Now?

The sale primarily involves the 53.4% stake controlled by Chairman Cha Myung-hoon, a former white-hat hacker who founded the exchange.

🇰🇷Coinone Put Up for Sale

Korea’s No.3 crypto exchange @CoinoneOfficial has been put up for sale, with Chairman Cha Myung-hoon considering selling part of his 53.44% stake, according to @sedaily_com.

The company is in talks with overseas exchanges and domestic financial firms,… pic.twitter.com/yQ8p2EA405

— ME Group (@MetaEraHK) January 26, 2026

His holdings are split between a 19.14% personal stake and 34.3% held through his company, The One Group.

Cha recently returned to active management after a brief four-month hiatus, a move many observers believe was designed to streamline the company for a potential transaction.

The timing may be driven by cooling valuations.

Coinone’s book value was reported at 75.2 billion won (approximately $52 million) at the end of the third quarter of 2025, a noticeable dip from its earlier valuation of 94.4 billion won.

This decline has also impacted the exchange’s second-largest shareholder, gaming firm Com2uS, which holds a 38.4% stake.

Having paid roughly $65 million for its position between 2021 and 2022, Com2uS is now looking at a book value significantly lower than its initial investment.

Is South Korea Becoming A Crypto M&A Hub?

South Korea remains a titan in the digital asset space.

By March 2025, the country boasted 16.29 million crypto investors, nearly 32% of its population, outnumbering the 14.23 million people invested in the local stock market.

🇰🇷 South Korea Goes All-In on Crypto! 🚀

📉 Stock market investors: 14.1M
📈 Crypto investors: 32% of the population!

🔹 Upbit: 9.2M users
🔹 Coinone: 3.2M users
🔹 Bithumb: 2.36M users

Regulation tightening as KuCoin & BitMEX face scrutiny.

Stay informed, stay secure! 🔐

— CoinMooner (@CoinMoonerX) April 3, 2025

This massive retail appetite has turned the country into a primary target for global and local conglomerates.

Coinone is far from the only exchange undergoing a major ownership shift.

In November 2025, internet giant Naver Financial completed a merger-acquisition deal to bring Dunamu, the operator of the country’s largest exchange Upbit, under its umbrella.

🚨NAVER FINANCIAL CONFIRMS MERGER WITH UPBIT

🇰🇷South Korea’s Naver Financial has confimed a stock-swap merger with Dunamu, the company behind Upbit. pic.twitter.com/h4npvkKFf5

— Coin Bureau (@coinbureau) November 26, 2025

After a two-year regulatory wait, Binance finally secured approval in October 2025 to acquire a majority stake in Gopax.

Financial heavyweight Mirae Asset Group has also moved to acquire Korbit in a deal estimated to be worth up to $100 million.

Can Product Innovation Save Falling Valuations?

Despite the financial pressures and shifting ownership, Coinone has been attempting to claw back market share through technical upgrades.

The exchange recently launched a flexible Bitcoin staking service, the first of its kind in the country, allowing users to earn rewards without the usual lock-up periods.

Chairman Cha has also pivoted the company toward integrating artificial intelligence to sharpen its competitive edge.

The potential arrival of Coinbase could provide the capital and global prestige needed to challenge the dominance of Upbit and Bithumb.

However, with regulators keeping a close watch on foreign ownership and market stability, any deal will likely face rigorous scrutiny before it can be finalised.

data.title: Clawdbot author: Billionaire Benhao's comeback is only due to the emptiness of retirement.
data.description:
The creator of Clawdbot, which has gone viral online, is actually a billionaire?!

I initially thought he was just another AI entrepreneur who dropped out of school, but after checking online, I found out that this guy started a business 15 years ago and is probably forty years old this year.

And his first venture took off immediately.

Started in 2011, cashed out and exited in 2021, making a hefty profit of100 million euros(approximately 830 million RMB). Logically, the script for achieving financial freedom should be: golf by day, wine and lobster by night. But guess what? After only four years of leisurely living, this guy couldn't sit still anymore. His hands were itching to get back to coding full-time, venturing into AI entrepreneurship. In less than a year, he created Clawdbot. A 24/7 open-source AI assistant that proactively reports to users like a personal secretary. This agent can do everything a human can—fill out forms, send emails, control browsers… a true "Jarvis." This phenomenal AI product, which went viral online, also helped Tim Cook boost Mac mini sales.

Netizens shouted: Absolute GOAT. Let's take a look at the legendary life of Clawdbot's creator, the "father of Jarvis". First Half: The Self-Made iOS Engineer The Boss is Peter Steinberger (let's call him Peter from now on). He's Austrian and graduated from the Vienna University of Technology with a degree in Computer and Information Science. In fact, even while still a student, Peter was already a seasoned iOS developer. During his studies, he provided technical consulting and one-on-one training for several well-known iOS projects. Even the first Mac/iOS development course at his alma mater was launched with Peter's involvement and promotion. In 2010, Peter received an order that would change his life thanks to his skills. The client wanted to create a magazine-style app based on PDF format. Peter spent eight weeks developing a complete iOS version for them. Initially, he didn't think much of it. But not long after, a colleague suddenly asked him, "Can I use this engine too?" Peter realized this was no small matter. At that time, the first-generation iPad had just been released, and applications were increasingly shifting experiences to the digital world. Paper documents were likely among the first to be restructured. Once documents were digitized, additional features such as electronic signatures, document viewing, editing, annotation, and collaboration needed to be added. Companies wanting to do these things themselves faced a high technical barrier. Therefore, in 2011, Peter launched a Solo project—PSPDFKit. The name is also very straightforward: P comes from Peter; PDF refers to PDF files; Kit represents the SDK toolkit. The project progressed exceptionally quickly. Peter built a website with an online store almost in a day and posted a message on Twitter. Several licenses were sold in the first week. However, at that time he didn't intend to start a business full-time. In fact, Peter had already accepted a programmer job in Silicon Valley. It was just that the work visa processing for the United States was exceptionally slow, taking a full nine months. But it was precisely these nine months that gave him an unexpected "window of opportunity." He didn't take on any other part-time jobs and devoted all his time to polishing PSPDFKit. Who could have imagined that this side hustle would progress so smoothly…? It was profitable almost from day one. By the time Peter officially joined the Silicon Valley company, the revenue generated by PSPDFKit had already exceeded his salary. Four months later, Peter returned to his hometown, going all-in on PSPDFKit, officially expanding the team, and bringing in two co-founders. As it turns out, he truly caught the wave: document processing tools, even those with features like "previewing cloud files," were incredibly difficult to implement at the time, yet essential for businesses. Now, PSPDFKit has laid the foundation, allowing businesses to directly integrate the SDK and API for a subscription fee. Peter's business naturally boomed. By 2021, PSPDFKit had become the de facto standard in the document processing field. Clients include well-known companies such as Apple, Adobe, Lufthansa, Dropbox, and Disney, indirectly serving nearly 1 billion consumers in 150 countries. Peter's life also took a turn that year. In October 2021, the renowned firm Insight Partners announced an investment in PSPDFKit. Peter chose this juncture to cash out and exit, selling most of his shares for approximately €100 million. Peter's Second Half But the story is only just beginning... Having achieved financial freedom, Peter felt an unprecedented emptiness. When I sold my shares in PSPDFKit, my heart was breaking. PSPDFKit was Peter's 13 years of hard work. But he was simply too tired and had to take a break, and the emergence of Insight Partners provided a valuable opportunity. After retiring, Peter indulged in revelry and pleasure, even moving to another country. But all of this only made him feel like a walking corpse. To fill the emptiness inside, he tried various forms of psychotherapy and even daphne—a traditional hallucinogenic drink originating from South America. Four years passed in this way. Until one day, Peter suddenly realized: moving cities and countries couldn't bring true happiness; the meaning of life might never be found. Because the true meaning—needs me to create it myself. One day, Peter suddenly had a new idea, and sat down at the computer to start writing code. At that moment, I realized that my spark had returned. He suddenly understood that putting his ideas into practice and writing code had always been the happiest thing in his life. So, in June 2025, Peter announced his comeback on his blog. On LinkedIn, his latest identity is the founder of Amantus Machina, dedicated to developing next-generation hyper-personalized AI agents. Six months later, Clawbot became a viral sensation online, becoming the first truly viral AI product of 2026. While listening to a podcast the other day, the founder of Lovart mentioned a point: In the mobile internet era, the head effect was too strong. Many excellent entrepreneurs of the previous generation didn't even have a chance to fully demonstrate their abilities before the window of opportunity closed. Many people are eager to try. But now, technology is tearing open an unprecedentedly huge window. The emergence of AI has finally given us the opportunity to completely remake all products. For many entrepreneurs of the previous generation, this was like a ticket that had been long overdue. Peter was thinking the same thing: We're back… it's time to make a big splash.

data.title: Japan Targets 2028 for Crypto ETFs as Regulators Weigh Investor Protections
data.description:
Why 2028 Is Emerging as the Target

Japan’s financial regulators are laying the groundwork for a major shift in crypto market access, with discussions underway that could allow spot cryptocurrency exchange-traded funds (ETFs) to launch as early as 2028, according to multiple reports citing people familiar with the matter.

Japan is jumping on the crypto ETF bandwagon🇯🇵

Japan is eyeing to approve its first crypto ETFs as early as 2028, with Nomura and SBI Holdings expected to launch the first lineup of funds.

This comes after US BTC ETFs accumulated $115B+ in net assets, and Hong Kong became… pic.twitter.com/udZCgEaIA0

— Danny Kunwoong Park (@ParkKunwoong) January 26, 2026

At the center of the proposal is Japan’s Financial Services Agency (FSA), which is considering changes to its regulatory framework that would classify cryptocurrencies as eligible ETF assets for the first time. If implemented, the move would end Japan’s longstanding ban on spot crypto ETFs and mark a significant recalibration of the country’s cautious approach to digital assets.

Regulators’ focus on 2028 reflects the complexity of rewriting Japan’s ETF and investor-protection rules rather than an imminent approval, according to Nikkei. Any change would require formal consultations, revisions to existing asset classification rules, and safeguards tailored to retail investors.

Under current regulations, ETFs in Japan are restricted to traditional asset classes, keeping direct crypto exposure outside the framework. By contrast, allowing spot crypto ETFs would enable funds holding assets like Bitcoin and Ether to list on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, offering investors regulated access through standard brokerage accounts without the need for wallets or private key management.

--LAW DAY 33--

Under the new Japan Law set to take effect next year, only licensed entities such as fund transfer service providers or trust banks can issue or distribute fiat-pegged stablecoins, a framework that has opened the door to regulated launches like RLUSD. https://t.co/yd1bjCIqQipic.twitter.com/fzIU3tgNJj

— Chad Steingraber (@ChadSteingraber) August 22, 2025

Nikkei estimates that Japan’s crypto ETF market could eventually reach 1 trillion yen in assets, though the figure remains speculative and dependent on final regulatory decisions and market conditions.

The extended timeline also reflects lessons learned from overseas markets. The United States and Hong Kong approved their first spot crypto ETFs in 2024, providing regulators with real-world data on custody models, market surveillance, and investor behavior that Japan appears keen to study before moving forward.

Japan is inching toward its first spot crypto ETFs—expected by 2028. Yeah, the wait feels eternal, but over 60% of local investors are itching for crypto exposure. Big players like Nomura and SBI are already prepping spicy ETF combos (gold-Bitcoin, Bitcoin-XRP). Regulators remain… pic.twitter.com/q2SraJYnYs

— Fama Crypto (@Famacrypt) January 26, 2026

Institutions Prepare as Demand Builds

Despite regulatory hurdles, Japan’s largest financial institutions are already positioning themselves for a potential launch. Nomura Holdings and SBI Holdings are widely seen as early candidates to issue the country’s first crypto-linked ETFs once rules are finalized.

SBI has previously disclosed plans for multiple products, including a Bitcoin–XRP ETF and a gold-and-crypto hybrid fund, each contingent on regulatory approval. In August 2025, a Nomura executive cited survey data showing that more than 60% of Japanese investors want exposure to crypto assets in some form, underscoring pent-up demand.

The push from industry coincides with broader policy signals from Tokyo. In early January, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama described 2026 as Japan’s “Digital Year One.”

"In the US, crypto assets are increasingly used via ETFs as inflation hedges, and Japan must also pursue advanced fintech initiative."

🚨BIG NEWS: 😳😳

SBI Holdings just applied to Japan’s FSA for TWO crypto ETFs featuring $XRP, Bitcoin, and gold-backed assets! 🇯🇵🔥

If approved, this would be Japan’s first regulated XRP ETF game changer for institutional adoption! 💥 pic.twitter.com/VrvC0DCWhW

— Xaif Crypto🇮🇳|🇺🇸 (@Xaif_Crypto) August 6, 2025

A Broader Shift in Japan’s Crypto Stance

While officials have stopped short of confirming a timeline, the ETF discussions form part of a wider reassessment of Japan’s digital asset policies. Proposed reforms under consideration include reducing crypto capital gains taxes, allowing banks and brokerages to hold digital assets, and reclassifying major cryptocurrencies as financial products.

🚨 🇯🇵 JAPAN SLASHES CRYPTO TAX FROM 55% TO 20%

I AM SPEECHLESS 😶👇 https://t.co/yFkFkK4QSOpic.twitter.com/QXfw8WetnE

— Pumpius (@pumpius) December 1, 2025

Approval of spot crypto ETFs would represent a clear break from Japan’s historically conservative posture and help the country keep pace with regional competitors. South Korea has signaled plans to introduce Bitcoin ETFs by 2026, increasing pressure on Japan to remain competitive as capital and innovation gravitate toward more accommodating jurisdictions.

For now, regulators emphasize that the 2028 target reflects intent rather than approval. But if realized, the framework could reshape how Japanese investors access crypto—moving digital assets further into the mainstream of the country’s financial system.

data.title: Why are gold and silver prices hitting new highs while cryptocurrencies continue to fall?
data.description:
On January 26, 2026, spot gold reached $5,100 per ounce, a surge of over $110, or more than 2%, during the day. Spot gold broke the $5,000 per ounce mark for the first time in history, just over 100 days after first breaking the $4,000 mark (October 8, 2025). Market analysts pointed out that macroeconomic factors such as increased gold purchases by central banks, geopolitical tensions, and economic uncertainty are the reasons driving up gold prices in recent years. Spot silver also followed suit, continuing its upward trend from last week, strongly reaching $110 per ounce during the day, just one trading day after breaking $100 last Friday, with a daily increase of about 6%. While precious metal prices soared, the cryptocurrency market was not optimistic. BTC was trading at $87,480.58, down 5.5% in the past 7 days. ETH was trading at $2,863.18, down 10.6% in the past 7 days. Why are gold and silver prices soaring while cryptocurrencies continue to fall?

Why are gold and silver prices soaring while cryptocurrencies continue to fall?

I. What is driving the surge in precious metal prices?

I. What is driving the surge in precious metal prices?

1. Geopolitical Risks

From geopolitical competition in Europe to military standoffs in the Middle East, multiple hot-spot events have intertwined, exacerbating risk aversion in global markets and becoming one of the core drivers pushing up gold prices.

1) Greenland Crisis

On January 25, Trump stated that the United States expects to gain "sovereignty" over the areas where US military bases are located on Greenland, and said that the relevant negotiations are "going well" and "we will get everything we want." It is understood that this does not involve the United States taking over Greenland completely, but rather making US military facilities, including the Pitufik Space Station, areas under US sovereign control. Greenland has clearly expressed its opposition, stating that the sovereignty issue is an "inviolable red line."

Going back further, on January 21, Trump stated that his goal of controlling Greenland "will never change" and refused to rule out the possibility of taking Greenland by force.

Trump clarified his objective on social media: to seize sovereignty over Greenland from NATO ally Denmark. The previous day, in an interview, Trump declined to comment on whether he would use force to seize Greenland, stating "no comment." On January 18, Trump announced tariffs of 10%-25% on EU goods, pressuring Denmark to sell Greenland, citing national security reasons. 2) Russia-Ukraine Conflict From January 23 to 24, local time, Russia, Ukraine, and the United States held their first trilateral security working group meeting in Abu Dhabi, the capital of the United Arab Emirates, since the escalation of the conflict in February 2022. All parties described the dialogue as "constructive," but core differences surrounding territorial issues such as the Donbas region remained sharp, and neither side showed any sign of backing down.

3) Escalating Tensions in the Middle East

On January 25, the US Navy's USS Abraham Lincoln Carrier Strike Group arrived in the Middle East and is conducting operations within the US Central Command's area of ​​responsibility. The US Air Force announced that it would soon begin a multi-day combat readiness exercise in the Middle East. The US Air Force stated that the exercise aims to demonstrate the US military's ability to deploy and maintain air combat power in the region. On the same day, Mohshinni Sani, a member of the Iranian Parliament's National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, stated that Iranian armed forces have entered a state of full alert in response to the current military deployments of the enemy.

2. Expectations of a Fed Rate Cut

A Fed rate cut will significantly reduce the opportunity cost of holding gold. A Fed rate cut will directly lower the risk-free rate of return on dollar assets, such as US Treasury yields and bank deposit rates. When the yields of these fixed-income assets decline, investors who choose to hold gold will forgo less interest income, meaning the opportunity cost of holding gold will be significantly reduced.

Trump has stated that the Fed should lower interest rates when the market is rising.

In the past, interest rates would fall when the economy was doing well. There's a real deadlock in the Fed. The Fed chairman has stifled every economic rebound. Powell is "soon to be gone." "The US (December) inflation data is out, very low! This means Jerome Powell should cut rates significantly! If he doesn't, he'll just continue to say 'too late'! Besides, the US economic growth data is also very good. Thanks, Mr. Tariff!" Brian Martin, head of G3 economy research at ANZ Bank, said the Fed might keep rates unchanged in January, but the view that the rate-cutting cycle is on a long-term pause lacks a reasonable basis. He expects the Fed to cut the federal funds target rate by 25 basis points in March, and another 25 basis points in June, bringing the target rate down to 3%-3.25% by mid-year. Martin pointed out that US inflation will gradually moderate in 2026. 3. Global Central Bank Gold Buying Spree On January 20th, the Polish central bank announced that it had approved a plan to purchase up to 150 tons of gold, which would increase the country's gold reserves to 700 tons. "This will put Poland among the top 10 countries with the largest gold reserves globally." According to data from the People's Bank of China, China's gold reserves at the end of December were 74.15 million ounces (approximately 2306.323 tons), an increase of 30,000 ounces (approximately 0.93 tons) month-on-month, marking the 14th consecutive month of gold purchases.

(Gold reserves of major gold-purchasing central banks as of November 2025. Source: World Gold Council)

In a report, Chris Weston, head of research at Pepperstone, pointed out that gold is increasingly seen as a tool to hedge against the "unpredictability" of Trump's policies. While many traders view gold as a hedge against the risk of a potential US-EU tariff war triggered by Trump's Greenland issue, he noted that even after the tariff threat has been lifted, gold's upward trend has not subsided.

Pepperstone's head of research, Chris Weston, pointed out in a report that gold is increasingly seen as a tool to hedge against the "unpredictability" of Trump's policies.

II. Why are cryptocurrencies continuing to fall?

1. The US government may shut down again

On January 10, Trump stated that another US government shutdown was possible on January 30. According to Polymarket data on January 26, traders predicted a 78% probability of another US government shutdown before the end of January, a probability that rose by 68% in the past 24 hours.

A US government shutdown would lead to a withdrawal of dollar liquidity from the market, causing a decline in risk appetite and triggering a withdrawal of funds from high-risk assets such as crypto, which would be detrimental to the price trend of the crypto market in the short term.

2. The CLARITY Act Fails

Previously, Coinbase had publicly and strongly opposed the existing version of the CLARITY Act.

On January 15, Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that after reviewing the Senate Banking Committee's draft cryptocurrency bill, Coinbase could not support this version of legislation.

Key issues include: a de facto ban on tokenized stocks; restrictions on DeFi, granting the government unlimited access to personal financial records and weakening privacy rights; weakening the CFTC's authority, stifling innovation, and subordinating it to the SEC; and amendments that could stifle stablecoin reward mechanisms and allow banks to block competitors. Armstrong points out that the draft is "worse than maintaining the status quo," preferring no bill to a bad one, but he remains optimistic about reaching a more reasonable version through continued collaboration and emphasizes that the crypto industry should receive the same regulatory treatment as traditional finance in the United States. For details, please see the Jinse Finance article "The Stablecoin Yield Debate Intensifies: CLARITY Becomes the Focus of the Crypto vs. Bank Game"

If the US government shuts down again, it will further increase the uncertainty surrounding the passage of the CLARITY Act. 3. Tightening Regulation
On January 20th, reports indicated that the Netherlands plans to tax unrealized capital gains on a range of investments, including stocks, bonds, and cryptocurrencies. This tax plan has drawn strong criticism from investors and cryptocurrency stakeholders, who warn that it could accelerate capital flight.

At the end of last year, the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) prepared to strengthen and centralize the regulation of the EU's crypto asset market, further unifying the implementation of the MiCA (Crypto Asset Market Regulation). Several national regulatory bodies, including the French Financial Supervisory Authority (AMF), have called for explicit stipulations within the MiCA framework that trading and execution activities (including local order books) must be based within the EU and subject to local regulation. This move by ESMA may mean prohibiting EU crypto platforms from sharing order books with non-EU, non-MiCA-regulated exchanges to ensure regulatory consistency.

Nicolas de Konin, a financial services lawyer at Norton Rothschild, believes that historically, cryptocurrency trading firms have shared liquidity with non-EU platforms, so any changes will be closely related to most cryptocurrency trading platforms, and ultimately to proprietary trading firms and many other participants in the market. “This will further facilitate price formation within the EU and impact order flow and overall liquidity. The larger the pool, the better the liquidity for both the platform and users. I think that mandating EU-only pools could lead to liquidity dispersion and widen spreads initially; the market will usually adapt, but this adjustment won't be immediate.” 4. Net Outflows from ETFs According to SoSoValue data, last week, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $1.33 billion, and Ethereum spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $611 million. Institutional redemptions of ETFs were seen as a bearish signal, triggering market concerns about a “large-scale capital withdrawal,” leading to retail and quantitative funds following suit and selling. Net outflows also lead to reduced market liquidity, widening bid-ask spreads, and even small transactions can trigger significant volatility, exacerbating market fragility and making investors more inclined to wait and see or withdraw, creating a vicious cycle.

III. BTC Market Outlook

  • Coinbase, in its Q1 2026 Cryptocurrency Performance Report, noted that a survey of 75 institutional investors and 73 independent investors conducted between early December and early January found that 71% of institutional investors and 60% of independent investors "believe Bitcoin is undervalued." Santiment points out that the cryptocurrency market is in a "phase of uncertainty, with retail investors withdrawing and funds and attention flowing to more traditional assets. Meanwhile, relatively calm signals such as supply distribution and a lack of social discussion suggest that a bottom may be forming. The best approach may be to wait patiently." Nik Bhatia, founder of the global macro research firm The Bitcoin Layer, believes that part of the reason for the low market sentiment may be the recent surge in metal prices. "Gold prices are approaching $5,000, and silver prices are $100. Because Bitcoin hasn't benefited from the rise in precious metals, market sentiment is extremely low, almost reminiscent of the bear market sentiment when Bitcoin prices fell to $17,000 after the FTX incident. I'm bullish on the market, but it's a painful kind of bullish sentiment; fear dominates, and you have to overcome it." IV. Is BTC a safe-haven asset? Safe-haven assets, also known as secure assets, refer to assets whose prices remain relatively stable or even rise against the trend during risk events such as economic downturns, increased market volatility, and geopolitical conflicts, helping investors avoid risks and preserve asset value. Their core characteristic is a low or negative correlation with risky assets. However, the cryptocurrency market is highly volatile; compared to gold and silver, its volatility means that Bitcoin is a high-risk asset. Alex Thorn, research director at crypto financial services firm Galaxy Digital, notes: “What we’re seeing now is geopolitical uncertainty, gold trading hitting new all-time highs, and Bitcoin underperforming. At least in terms of real-time performance, Bitcoin hasn’t fully lived up to its design purpose.” Bitunix analyst Dean Chen notes: “Bitcoin is currently in a state of split narrative. Among cryptocurrency native investors, it’s increasingly seen as a geopolitical hedging tool and a non-sovereign store of value. However, for the broader market, Bitcoin is still primarily viewed as a high-beta risk asset.” Campbell Harvey of Duke University points out: While the two assets share some common characteristics, the unique aspects of cryptocurrencies—such as Bitcoin’s existential threats like potential blockchain attacks—make it “unlikely to replace gold as the preferred safe-haven asset.” Other crypto market observers argue: Investment decisions are often based on belief, so if you can convince enough people that Bitcoin is a safe-haven asset, it will become one. The problem is, far fewer people believe in Bitcoin than impulsive gamblers and scammers. Therefore, instead of serving as a safe haven, it's more like a slot machine. People are now flocking to gold because they believe inflation might rebound, and gold tends to outperform inflation in the long run. While Bitcoin (Buttcoin) has indeed outperformed inflation, this is only because it's a speculative asset; its real value isn't much higher than Tesla stock, and may even be lower. For truly savvy investors, gold is a good hedge against declining bond yields and stock market uncertainty, while cryptocurrencies are not, and never will be.

data.title: NFT Marketplace Nifty Gateway to End Operations After Nearly Six Years in the Market
data.description:
One of the digital art world’s most iconic pillars is preparing to turn off the lights.

Nifty Gateway, the platform that once turned NFTs into a household name by hosting million-dollar drops from stars like Beeple and Grimes, will officially shut down on 23 February 2026.

The marketplace has already shifted into a withdrawal-only phase, giving its remaining users a 30-day window to rescue their digital artwork and cash before the interface disappears forever.

Received notification that @niftygateway was closing. Apparently only 30 days are provided for withdrawing our assets. I now see that @Gemini is very insistent on scraping assets from users and providing no support whatsoever. Disappointed in this carelessness. pic.twitter.com/tqCOnbJu9C

— Chain Ranger Galaxius 🌌🪐💫 (@CRGalaxius) January 25, 2026

Why Is Nifty Gateway Closing Now?

The decision to pull the plug comes from its parent company, the Gemini exchange, which purchased the platform back in 2019.

In a move to consolidate its offerings, Gemini is folding its NFT ambitions into a single "super app" strategy.

Today, we are announcing that the Nifty Gateway platform will be closing on February 23, 2026. Starting today, Nifty Gateway is in withdrawal-only mode.

Nifty Gateway was launched in 2020 with the vision of revolutionizing digital art. Since launching, Nifty supported dozens of…

— Nifty Gateway Studio (@niftygateway) January 24, 2026

While the standalone Nifty Gateway website will vanish, the underlying technology isn't being abandoned entirely.

The company explained in a recent blog post,

“This decision will allow Gemini to sharpen its focus and execute on the vision of building a one-stop super app for customers.”

For those still attached to their digital collections, Gemini plans to continue supporting these assets through its own dedicated Gemini Wallet, which was introduced in August 2025.

I hope @Gemini will keep maintaining @niftygateway servers because all their NFT metadata are stored there. If not, collections from @SamSpratt or @beeple will be broken with not possibility of recovery.

— G4SP4RD (@G4SP4RD) January 25, 2026

From Market Leader To Studio Pivot

Nifty Gateway was a trailblazer when it arrived in 2020.

At a time when buying crypto art required complex digital wallets and technical know-how, Nifty opened the gates to everyone by accepting simple credit card payments.

This approach fueled a massive speculative wave, with the platform processing a staggering $300 million in sales during its peak in mid-2021.

However, as the speculative frenzy of the early 2020s cooled, the platform struggled to maintain its gravity.

By April 2024, the company had already tried to reinvent itself as Nifty Gateway Studio, moving away from a public marketplace to focus on high-end creative projects and corporate partnerships.

The platform, Nifty Gateway, which once facilitated over $300 million in sales, had shifted its focus to building onchain creative projects in 2024, but will now close.
What you think?
Tell us Below!
Read Here ---> https://t.co/OPjGiJDPJV
Francisco Rodrigues #Cryptocurrencypic.twitter.com/W5RiV8a2ye

— ⚠️💖 ᗰᗩᑌGIᗴ $MG 💖⚠️ (@Maugie_Games) January 24, 2026

Despite the rebrand, the platform never quite recaptured the lightning-in-a-bottle energy of its early years.

What Does The Future Hold For Digital Collectibles?

The exit of such a major player highlights the changing landscape of the blockchain world.

While the broader NFT market capitalization sat at a dizzying $17 billion in 2022, current valuations are closer to $2.8 billion.

There have been flashes of life; the first two weeks of January 2026 saw a 20% rebound, with total valuations climbing from $2.5 billion to over $3 billion.

The NFT market’s total value gained 25% in the first two weeks of the year.

The market capitalization stands at USD2.6 billion (PHP195 billion) with “blue chip” NFTs leading the way.

Bored Ape Yacht Club (BAYC) is closing in on a 6 ETH floor, while Pudgy Penguins has held above… pic.twitter.com/n3zISduLxe

— YGG Alerts (@YGGAlerts) January 15, 2026

Selective investment is still happening, but the focus has shifted.

Earlier this month, Animoca Brands acquired the gaming collectibles firm Somo, signaling that the industry is moving toward utility and gaming rather than just digital images.

Animoca Brands has acquired SOMO ⚡️

📑 About:@playsomo is a mobile game focused on monsters battling on the Abstract network.

🤝 Acquired by:@animocabrandspic.twitter.com/zc8e1IdxxK

— Fundraising Digest (@CryptoRank_VCs) January 14, 2026

As the era of the massive consumer marketplace ends, the technology is being tucked away into more specialised ecosystems.

Moving Assets Before The February Deadline

For the collectors still holding ether, US dollar balances, or NFTs on the site, the clock is ticking.

Nifty Gateway has confirmed it will send specific instructions via email to help users navigate the exit.

Assets can be moved either to a linked Gemini Exchange account or sent directly to a bank account through a Stripe integration.

While the company expressed deep gratitude for the community's pioneering contributions, the platform is scheduled to go offline next month, requiring users to adhere to jurisdictional regulations and relocate their assets before the 23 February 2026 deadline.

data.title: Why is Clawdbot trending? Is a self-hosting revolution on the horizon?
data.description:
Author: Haotian

Wow, everyone's talking about Clawdbot. Although the name includes "Bot," after careful research, I found that this thing is definitely not just a chatbot wrapped in an LLM shell. It might spark a "self-hosted revolution" in the era of Agentic AI!

1) A Paradigm Shift from "Passive Response" to "Proactive Execution"

The core difference of Clawdbot lies in its Agentic attribute. Through Cron Jobs and Hooks mechanisms, it transforms the role of AI from "consultant" to "executive employee."

In other words, it's no longer a chat box waiting for you to type "Prompt," but a truly 24/7 "digital employee."

For example, it can automatically scan Twitter trending topics every 4 hours and summarize them to your Telegram, or monitor MEME trending topics for large capital inflows every 15 minutes and synchronize them with you in real time, etc. Logically, this kind of proactive SaaS-based AI product is difficult to popularize because no commercial company can afford the cost of providing "unlimited polling" computing power tokens for each user. Clawdbot essentially "decentralizes" this type of computing power cost to users who need it through self-hosting. As long as you are willing to pay for its API and electricity, it can work for you 24/7, which is exactly the same logic as running a company and employing employees in reality; 2) It is a "middleware" that breaks down APP data silos. I think this is another smart move by Anthropic to turn a strategic disadvantage into an advantage after MCP. You see, everyone's vying for the Agentic OS market. The recent blockbuster deal between Gemini and Apple is based on the logic of using complementary hardware and software to become the user gateway in the Agentic era. Clawdbot, however, doesn't try to compete in the all-encompassing OS arena—in fact, it can't compete—it only acts as a connector. It integrates WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, and Slack via APIs, and even has built-in browser control. This means it can not only "read" your messages, but also simulate mouse and keyboard input to operate web pages and access your local files. It feels like Clawdbot is essentially acting as an "execution layer" between user intent and closed Web2 applications. Moreover, this execution isn't purely C-end entry logic. Users can manage their digital employees without the convenience of Siri, even with multiple navigations, resulting in exceptionally strong user stickiness. 3) The AI ​​Era's "Not your keys, not your coins" Clawdbot adheres to a "Local-First" strategy. Whether running on a Mac Mini or a Raspberry Pi, it emphasizes the localization of core logic and memory. While underlying inference might still require calling Claude's API, your task flow, preferences, and file indexes are all in your own hands. This is incredibly important. Just a few days ago, I was saying that building a localized environment with Claude Code + Skills + Cowork is the future. The advent of Clawdbot has accelerated this pace. Time to grab a Mac Mini! What are you waiting for? The logic is simple: in the AI ​​era, "data sovereignty" will be a major issue. Think about it: when AI penetrates core areas like financial monitoring and workflow automation, this hybrid architecture of "local brain + cloud computing power" will inevitably become the main trend. That's all. In conclusion, I believe that the emergence of Clawdbot will rapidly drag "AI toolification" into the realm of "productization logic," and truly become an essential infrastructure for future super-individuals to enhance their personal productivity.

data.title: Can the privacy sector finally gain recognition? Three potential areas worth watching.
data.description:
Author: Kevin, Movemaker Researcher; Source: X, @MovemakerCN

In 2026, the crypto industry is standing at a new crossroads. With the deep involvement of institutional capital, the settling of regulatory frameworks, and the growth of AI in the on-chain economy, the privacy track is undergoing an unprecedented paradigm shift. Over the past decade, blockchain privacy technology has often been misunderstood as a money laundering tool or a safe haven for hackers. This early form based on "indiscriminate anonymity" has repeatedly suffered setbacks due to its inherent antagonism with the global financial regulatory system. However, the Privacy 2.0 paradigm, centered on "programmable compliance," has taken shape and is becoming the cornerstone of the next generation of Web3 infrastructure.

1. The "Lost Decade" and the Logic of Potential Explosion in the Privacy Sector

1.1 The Misconception of Indiscriminate Anonymity

Looking back at the past decade (2014-2024), the development of the blockchain privacy sector has been filled with idealism, but this has also led to its quagmire in commercialization. Early privacy projects, such as Monero and early Zcash, were deeply influenced by the Cypherpunk spirit, and their core design philosophy was "adversarial privacy." This thinking holds that privacy should be absolute and indiscriminate, and any form of regulatory backdoor or audit interface is a betrayal of the spirit of privacy.

However, this technical path has been proven to be a false proposition in practice. Its core contradiction lies in the fact that it attempts to build a completely black-box value transfer network in a system that needs to interact with the real-world financial system.

  • Technical Path Deviation: Most Privacy 1.0 projects equate privacy with "hiding the transaction graph." For example, coin mixers sever the connection between senders and receivers by disrupting the flow of funds. This "privacy for privacy's sake" approach, while mathematically achieving anonymity, creates significant obstacles at the compliance level.

  • The Deadlock in Commercial Expansion: Due to a lack of compliance boundaries, early privacy coins struggled to be accepted by mainstream exchanges and payment gateways. Under pressure from anti-money laundering compliance, leading exchanges such as Coinbase and Binance delisted privacy coins in multiple jurisdictions. This means that privacy assets lost their liquidity entry point, becoming niche speculative tools or black market mediums, unable to support large-scale commercial activities.

1.2 The Lost Decade: A Mismatch Between Technology and the Market

The past decade has been called the "lost decade" for the privacy sector because the industry invested too many resources in the wrong direction. Due to an overemphasis on "permissionless anonymity," most technological innovations (such as early ring signatures and simple zk-SNARKs transfers) focused on how to hide data more deeply, rather than how to manage data more flexibly.

This mismatch led to two results:

  • Lack of Application Scenarios: Aside from transfers and speculation, privacy coins have hardly built a rich ecosystem similar to DeFi, NFTs, or DAOs. This is because building complex financial applications on privacy chains that do not support programmability is extremely difficult.

  • Regulatory Arbitrage Failed: Attempts to circumvent regulation through decentralized architectures have completely failed. Regulators easily curbed the expansion of these protocols by cracking down on deposit and withdrawal channels and infrastructure service providers.

    • 1.3 Defining "Programmable Compliance"

    Entering 2025, the privacy sector experienced a structural resurgence. This was not due to increased market demand for "anonymity," but rather a qualitative shift in the connotation of privacy technology. A new generation of privacy projects is building what is known as Privacy 2.0, whose core feature is "programmable compliance."

    "Programmable compliance" refers to embedding compliance logic (such as identity verification, blacklist screening, transaction limits, and audit report generation) directly into the underlying code or smart contracts of privacy protocols. It allows users to prove the legality of their actions to specific regulators or counterparties while keeping their data confidential from the public. This paradigm shift manifests in three levels: **Optional Privacy:** The system may be transparent by default, or allow users to selectively enable privacy protection, moving away from a blanket mandatory anonymity. **Auditability:** Users possess a "View Key" that they can proactively share with tax authorities, enabling them to decrypt and verify the transaction history of a specific account without disclosing it to the entire network. **Rule Embedding:** Compliance rules can be enforced at the protocol level. For example, a privacy-focused stablecoin contract could stipulate that "only addresses holding compliant DIDs (decentralized identities) can receive transfers," or that "single transfers exceeding $10,000 require additional compliance documentation."

    1.4 The Inevitable Need for RWA Asset Tokenization

    The tokenization of Real-World Assets (RWA) is one of the biggest growth drivers in the Crypto industry in 2026. However, the large-scale on-chaining of RWA faces an irreconcilable "paradox."

    • Institutional Pain Points: Financial giants like BlackRock and Goldman Sachs would never trade on a completely public ledger like Ethereum. This would not only leak trade secrets but also lead to market scrambling and targeted short/long squeezes.

      • 1.4 The Inevitable Need for RWA Asset Tokenization

      The tokenization of Real-World Assets (RWA) is one of the biggest growth drivers in the Crypto industry in 2026. However, the large-scale on-chaining of RWA faces an irreconcilable "paradox."

      • Institutional Pain Points: Financial giants like BlackRock and Goldman Sachs would never trade on a completely public ledger like Ethereum. This would not only leak trade secrets but also lead to market scrambling and targeted short/long squeezes.

      • The Contradiction of Regulation: Regulatory agencies (such as the SEC and ESMA) require financial transactions to be traceable and monitorable (meeting AML requirements), but at the same time, data protection regulations (such as the EU's GDPR) strictly prohibit the public disclosure of customers' sensitive personal data on an immutable blockchain.

      Privacy 2.0 is the only solution. Only through privacy technology with "programmable compliance" can "confidentiality to the public and transparency to regulators" be achieved.

      1.5 The Underlying Architecture of the AI ​​Agent Era: Protecting Algorithms and Strategies

      2026 is considered the first year of the "AI Agent Economy." Thousands of autonomous AI agents will conduct transactions, arbitrage, and resource allocation on the blockchain. For these AI agents, privacy is no longer an option, but the foundation of their survival.

    **Policy Protection:** The core competitiveness of an AI Agent lies in its algorithm and trading strategy. If executed on a public ledger, its intent and pattern can be instantly identified and targeted by MEV bots (sandwich attacks). Only in a privacy environment (such as Anoma's encrypted intent pool) can an Agent securely publish trading intents without exposing its strategy. **Model Privacy:** Future AI Agents may carry proprietary model weights or inference logic. Through zkVM (such as services provided by Boundless), Agents can generate "proof of inference" demonstrating that their output is calculated based on a specific model without revealing the model itself. This is known as zkML and is key to the assetization of AI models. 1.6 Shift in Regulatory Attitude: From Suppression to Regulation The attitudes of regulatory agencies also underwent a subtle but significant shift in 2025-2026. Represented by the EU's AMLR and MiCA, major global jurisdictions began to differentiate between "Anonymity-Enhancing Tools" and "Privacy-Enhancing Technologies (PETs)." The former were considered money laundering tools and faced continued crackdown. The latter were seen as compliance infrastructure and were encouraged. Discussions by agencies such as the US Treasury Department also indicated that regulators were beginning to realize that the data breach risks associated with fully transparent ledgers were themselves a systemic risk. Therefore, the technological path supporting "selective disclosure" gradually gained acceptance.

    2. Representatives of Three Sub-directions in the Privacy Track

    Therefore, the explosion of the privacy track is no longer just a hype, but an inevitable result of industry evolution. Following this line of thought, let's look at the strategic positioning of the following three projects:

    2.1 Infrastructure/Computing Layer: Zama

    2.2 Transaction Matching Layer: Anoma

    If Zama solves the problem of data confidentiality, then Anoma focuses on solving the discovery and matching mechanism of transactions

    Traditional DeFi users' transactions are usually exposed in the Mempool, with their intentions fully revealed to the MEV bot. Anoma addresses this by allowing counterparties to discover privacy; users publish encrypted intentions, and the Solver matches the transactions without decryption (combined with FHE or TEE). It's clear that this solution addresses privacy issues at an entry level; it also tackles the problems of multi-chain fragmentation and interaction complexity. Once a user's transaction intent is publicly disclosed in Mempool, it becomes prey for MEV bots. Anoma introduces an "intent center" architecture. Users no longer sign transactions, but instead sign "intents." Intents can be encrypted. They are only decrypted and executed when specific conditions are met. This means that MEV bots cannot peek into a user's hand and therefore cannot launch preemptive attacks. Anoma recently completed its integration with the Base mainnet, extending its intent settlement layer to the EVM ecosystem, thereby reaching a wider liquidity market. The current technological focus is on the final activation of the "protocol adapter." As a core component connecting user intent and Solver execution, the launch of this adapter will mark the complete closed loop of network functionality. At that time, $XAN will transform from a simple governance credential into a utility token used to pay network settlement fees. Whether it can effectively support the matching needs of multi-chain transactions is worth paying attention to.

    2.3 Validation Layer: Boundless

    With the explosive growth of ZK Rollups and various privacy applications, the computationally intensive "Proving" process has become the biggest obstacle.

    ... Boundless was created to address this need, specializing in the large-scale production of "universal privacy proofs." Boundless transforms the ability to generate ZK proofs into a tradable computing power commodity, modularly embedding it into all scenarios with a thirst for ZK verification. **Operating Mechanism:** This is a free market for computing power. Developers post computing needs (such as verifying privacy ledgers or running AI models), and Provers in the network enter the market with GPU/FPGA computing power to bid for the orders, generate proofs, and ultimately upload them to the blockchain. **Unified Interface:** It's like Chainlink in the ZK computing field, providing a standardized proof service layer for all blockchains. Any chain can "outsource" its heavy ZK workload simply through API calls. As demand for ZK-Rollups and ZK Coprocessors explodes, Boundless will become the decentralized engine handling massive volumes of ZK proofs. It completely unlocks the limits of imagination for privacy applications: whether it's on-chain identity, credit assessment, compliance auditing, or AI Agent policy verification, zkVM can provide a privacy-protecting shield. Boundless leverages BitVM to upgrade Bitcoin to the ultimate settlement layer for ZK proofs. This is not merely a technological upgrade, but a strategic leap: it's no longer limited to being a coprocessor within the Ethereum ecosystem. Once this decentralized Prover Market is operational, Boundless will anchor high-load AI inference and complex financial model computations to the absolute security of Bitcoin. 3. If the privacy sector gains legitimacy, the moat effect may be amplified. Having understood the strategies of Zama, Anoma, and Boundless, we need to examine the ultimate outcome of the privacy sector from a higher perspective. Privacy will create the most powerful "chain-locked" effect. 3.1 Differences in Network Effects between Public and Privacy Chains. On public chains, the migration costs for users and assets are extremely low. Because all data is public and transparent, cross-chain bridges can easily verify states and transfer assets. This leads to competition between public chains other than Ethereum, Solana, and BNB becoming a simple competition of performance and fees, resulting in shallow moats. Users are "mercenaries," going wherever the profits are highest. However, on privacy chains, the situation is completely different. 3.2 “Chain Locking” Mechanism: Why Privacy is Difficult to Achieve Across Chains “Cross-chain tokens are easy, but cross-chain privacy is difficult.” When a user accumulates a large amount of encrypted state (such as encrypted credit records, private transaction history, and undisclosed holding strategies) in a privacy ecosystem (such as a chain based on Zama fhEVM), it is difficult for them to migrate these “secrets” to another chain. Metadata Leakage Risk: Even if cross-chain migration is technically possible, the migration process itself will generate metadata. Monitors can infer a user's true identity by analyzing characteristics such as the time, amount, and frequency of fund inflows and outflows. This is referred to as the "deanonymization risk of deposits and withdrawals." **State non-portability:** Encrypted data often relies on the key management system of a specific chain (such as Zama's threshold decryption network). To migrate data, it must first be decrypted (exposing the risk) and then encrypted on another chain. This high risk cost constitutes an extremely high barrier to entry. This "easy to get in, hard to get out" (or "afraid to leave") characteristic creates strong user stickiness. **Stronger network effects:** In privacy networks, the more users, the larger the anonymity set, and the stronger the privacy protection for each user. This is a positive cycle: better privacy -> more users -> better privacy.

  • Winner-Takes-All: Due to extremely high migration costs and strong network effects, the privacy sector is unlikely to see a "hundred flowers blooming" scenario, but is more likely to evolve into a "winner-takes-all" situation. A few leading privacy infrastructures will capture the vast majority of the value.

    • The privacy sector in 2026 is an essential step for Web3 to move towards a mature financial system. Reborn from the ruins of Tornado Cash is a new generation of Privacy 2.0 forces equipped with FHE (computation), Intents, and zkVM (proof). The core driving force of this transformation is "programmable compliance," which aligns with the era of RWA asset on-chaining and the explosion of AI agents.

data.title: a16z-Backed Crypto Startup Entropy Shuts Down After Four Years and Returns $25 Million to Investors
data.description:
A Quiet Exit From The Crypto Scene

Tux Pacific, the founder of Entropy, has decided to pull the plug on the crypto custody and automation startup after four years of navigating a volatile market.

Despite securing substantial backing from industry giants like Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), the company is winding down operations and returning its remaining capital to investors.

The move follows two rounds of layoffs and several attempts to find a sustainable business model, eventually leading Pacific to conclude that the venture lacked the scale required to succeed in the long term.

Why Did A Twenty Five Million Dollar Venture Shut Down

Entropy entered the market in late 2021 with the ambitious goal of offering a decentralized alternative to established custodians like Coinbase and Fireblocks.

In June 2022, the startup raised $25 million in a seed round led by a16z, with participation from Coinbase Ventures, Dragonfly Capital, and Naval Ravikant.

However, the path to a viable product proved difficult.

a year ago i suggested @entropydotxyz build on @ikadotxyz because it will take them a fraction of the time and effort and they can’t compete with anything built on us

today they shut down after 5 years, raising $27m from @a16zcrypto & @dragonfly_xyz, and not launching anything

— d3h3d (ZUD arc) 「🦑」 (@d3h3d_) January 24, 2026

Most recently, the team shifted focus toward a crypto automation platform, a blockchain-native version of Zapier, using threshold cryptography and AI to streamline workflows.

Pacific explained on X,

"After an initial feedback request revealed that the business model wasn’t venture scale, I was left with the choice to find a creative way forward or pivot once more."

Rather than burning through the remaining funds on another gamble, the founder chose to exit.

I am winding-up Entropy.

After four years, several pivots, and two rounds of layoffs, I’ve decided to wind-up Entropy and return capital to our investors.

For the latter half of 2025, the Entropy team was hard at work on a crypto automations platform (basically n8n/zapier/etc…

— tux pacific (@__tux) January 24, 2026

How Hard Was The Journey For Entropy

The startup’s journey was marked by constant evolution, yet it struggled to capture the necessary market fit.

Pacific, an anarchist and self-taught cryptographer with a background at NuCypher, noted that the decision was the result of a grueling four-year stint.

Pacific shared,

"After four hard years working in crypto, I decided that the best I could do has already been done: it was time to close up shop."

While returning capital is relatively rare in the startup world, where many founders choose to pivot until the bank account is empty.

It reflects a pragmatic response to a 2025 funding environment that became increasingly selective, favoring late-stage companies over struggling early-stage projects.

What Is Next For Tux Pacific

In an unexpected twist, Pacific is leaving the digital asset space entirely to pursue a career in pharmaceutical research.

The focus of this new chapter will be on biophysics and organic chemistry, specifically looking at hormone delivery innovations for menopausal and transgender women.

Tux Pacific is a self-taught cryptographer and "market post-left-ish anarchist" who gained prominence as a visible transgender advocate in the technology sector.

Pacific intends to validate research on new drug formulations for estradiol, viewing this transition as a natural evolution of a career dedicated to innovation.

"My time in crypto might be coming to an end, as I feel myself drawn specifically into pharmaceuticals."

To Pacific, "a career is a practice: the goal is not the destination, but the journey of innovation."

Is This A Growing Trend Among Backed Projects

Entropy is not the only high-profile project recently handing back funds.

The decentralized social protocol Farcaster also announced it would return $180 million to investors following a takeover by Neynar.

While the circumstances differ, farcaster is continuing under new management while entropy is dissolving.

Both cases highlight a shift in how venture-backed entities handle capital when original goals are no longer reachable.

Pacific expressed gratitude for the support received during this transition, particularly from a16z general partner Guy Wuollet, describing their guidance as "invaluable" throughout the process of winding down the business.

data.title: Clawdbot, a sensation in Silicon Valley, allows AI to perform tasks with a single command.
data.description:
In recent weeks, a Clawdbot craze has swept through the X developer community. From GitHub commits posted at 2 a.m. to screenshots boasting "finally running!" late at night, this open-source project featuring a lobster emoji is becoming increasingly well-known.

（Source: Clawbot）

Some people have bought one or even multiple Mac Minis specifically for this purpose, keeping them running day and night in their homes. Others share how they use Telegram on Discord channels.

Remotely controlling a computer to deploy code has led to discussions among lawyers about its potential impact on the legal profession. Tech blogger Federico Viticci even documented his experience in detail on MacStories. He used Clawbot to consume 180 million Anthropic API Tokens in a week, concluding, "After using this superpower, I can never go back." (Source: X) What exactly is Clawdbot? Why is it generating so much excitement in the tech world? To be precise, Clawdbot is not a chatbot, but an AI Agent Gateway. Unlike tools like ChatGPT or Claude that require you to open a webpage, type a question, and wait for a reply, Clawdbot's design logic is: issue a command through a commonly used messaging application (currently supporting Telegram, WhatsApp, iMessage, or Discord, etc.), and it will invoke a large language model running in the background (such as Claude or Gemini) to translate your request into a local shell script and execute it on your computer.

Image | Sending instructions to Clawdbot using imessage (Source: Federico Viticci)

In other words, it doesn't tell you what to do, but rather does a task for you directly.

In other words, it doesn't tell you what to do, but rather does a task for you directly.

Image | Peter Steinberger (Source: X)

In late 2025, Steinberger decided to open-source his personal AI assistant "Clawdis" and renamed the project Clawdbot.

Within just a few weeks, the number of stars on GitHub surpassed 23k, and the Discord community grew from zero to over 5,000 members, with a nascent skills plugin ecosystem called ClawdHub taking shape. Community members contributed various plugins (skills) ranging from WhatsApp voice message transcription to automated website deployment.

（Source: GitHub）

Everything seems perfect, but Clawdbot still has one unavoidable problem: cost. Clawdbot itself is open-source and free, but it relies on the Large Language Model API to operate, and these API calls are billed in tokens. Federico Viticci, mentioned earlier, consumed 180 million tokens in a week. According to Anthropic's pricing, input tokens cost approximately $3 per million, and output tokens cost approximately $15 per million, meaning his bill could reach hundreds or even thousands of dollars. On Reddit, some users complained that "token usage is insane; it can use up the five-hour limit of a $200 Claude Pro plan in just one hour." The official documentation recommends a budget of $10-30 per month for light users, $30-70 for moderate users, and $70-150 or more for heavy users. This is a significant expense for users who want Clawdbot running 24/7 and handling complex tasks. A deeper issue is the limit of memory. While Clawdbot stores all conversations and learned information in a local file called MEMORY.md, theoretically ensuring it "never forgets," this file becomes increasingly bloated over time. When it expands to thousands or even tens of thousands of lines, it may lead to a consequence that has not yet been fully discussed: context rot. When AI needs to retrieve relevant content from massive amounts of historical information, the response speed may slow down, the accuracy may decrease, and there may even be confusion or errors in recall. Although RAG technology can alleviate this problem to some extent, whether this system can still be efficient after users have accumulated several months or even a year of usage data remains to be verified. Moreover, although many articles claim that "basic installation can be completed in 20-30 minutes," the actual threshold for ordinary users is higher than imagined. You'll need to install Node.js 22+, configure a Nix environment, obtain and correctly set up an Anthropic or OpenAI API key, manage credentials in macOS Keychain, understand how to securely access the Gateway, and grant your application shell script execution permissions. This might not be difficult for developers, but for ordinary users, it can be considered a significant technical gap. Excellent automation examples, such as real-time monitoring of abnormal trading volume in the options market, automatically publishing to five social media platforms and optimizing titles, and building a complete website and migrating data, all require hours or even days of custom development. One point repeatedly emphasized in the documentation is that while Clawdbot's basic functions (file management, simple research, and schedule lookup) are indeed usable out of the box, more advanced skills require building custom skills, integrating third-party APIs, and repeated testing and debugging. Another concern is the security risks associated with "localization." Giving AI the highest privileges to execute terminal commands means it can read your files, install software, modify system configurations, and access cookies and passwords saved in your browser. All of this is necessary for Clawdbot to function, but it also creates a huge attack surface. A widely circulated security warning on X points out the risk of "prompt injection": Suppose you ask Clawdbot to summarize a PDF file, and this file contains a piece of malicious text. Because large language models cannot reliably distinguish between "content to be analyzed" and "instructions to be executed," these malicious commands could potentially be executed. Developer Steinberger himself acknowledges in his security documentation: "Running intelligent agents is risky; please harden your configuration." So, is Clawdbot worth trying? The answer depends on your expectations. If you're looking for a voice assistant like Siri that works out of the box without any configuration, Clawdbot isn't for you. If you just want to occasionally ask AI questions, ChatGPT or the web version of Claude might suffice. But if you're a developer, researcher, content creator, or professional who handles a lot of repetitive tasks, and are willing to spend a few hours learning how to configure and gradually build your own automated workflows, then its capabilities are truly exciting: it might actually become a personalized, local digital assistant that remembers your preferences and habits, working 24/7.
